concerns.text: Tasks T034-T037 attempt to implement 'ontological reality' and 'statistical ghost' metrics. The spec (FR-001 to FR-007) and SCs define only statistical bias (Hellinger distance, absolute difference) and convergence flags. There is no functional requirement or success criterion for detecting 'ontological loss' or distinguishing 'statistical variance' from 'ontological reality'. These tasks introduce untestable scope creep that violates the spec's defined boundaries.

FR-004 explicitly requires flagging a run as 'inconclusive' if the Gelman-Rubin statistic ≥ 1.01 OR the 5000-step limit is reached. Task T018 only mentions flagging if the MCMC 'fails to converge', omitting the explicit hard-step limit condition. This is a semantic weakening of the FR-004 acceptance criteria.

Spec US-1, Scenario 3 requires the system to log a warning containing the 'segment ID' when missing segments are encountered. No task in Phase 3 explicitly implements the logic to detect missing segments, extract their IDs, and log them. This acceptance criterion is dropped from the task list.

SC-001 requires measuring Hellinger distance against 'intrinsic statistical uncertainty' to determine if bias is distinguishable from noise. Task T023 only implements the calculation of the distance itself. There is no corresponding task to compute or retrieve the 'intrinsic statistical uncertainty' baseline, leaving the success criterion unmeasurable.

SC-004 requires measuring consistency by calculating the 'standard deviation of the identified thresholds'. Task T030 describes calculating the standard deviation of thresholds but fails to define what constitutes the 'identified threshold' (e.g., specific Hz rate vs. binary pass/fail). This ambiguity prevents the task from satisfying the SC's requirement for a specific measurable outcome.
